title: claude-dotfiles — Machine Configuration
description: Machine-specific configuration. Maps canonical variable names to local paths on this machine. Never commit this file — paths are unique to this machine.

name.description: A short, memorable name for this machine. Used in logs and status output.

os.description: Operating system. Determines which shell commands Claude generates for this machine.

home.description: Absolute path to your home directory on this machine.

project_root.description: Where your code lives — git repositories, source files, compiled artefacts, and build outputs. This is for things you build, not things you write.

knowledge_root.description: Parent folder whose immediate subdirectories are all knowledge directories. All subdirectories are auto-discovered and named by their folder name. Use this when all your knowledge directories share a common parent. Combine with knowledge_dirs for any that don't.

knowledge_dirs.description: Named knowledge directories — where your thinking lives. Notes, docs, todos, tasks, plans, research. Each key becomes an uppercase variable in Claude sessions (e.g. 'notes' → $NOTES). Values are absolute paths on this machine. Use instead of, or alongside, knowledge_root.

additionalProperties.description: Absolute path to this knowledge directory on this machine.
